title: Aamund Jonson Forthun Born
description: The earliest known Fortney ancestor is born at the Forthun farm in Luster parish, Sogn, Norway. He will serve as farm manager of Bnr #1.

title: Synneva Clausdotter Born
description: Synneva Clausdotter is born at Forthun. Her marriage to Knut Aamundson in 1653 will bring the crucial farm parcel Bnr #8 into the family.

title: Bnr #8 Enters the Family
description: Synneva Clausdotter marries Knut Aamundson Forthun, bringing the farm parcel Bruks Nummer Otte (Bnr #8) into the family. This parcel will remain in the family for 255 years.

title: Knut Torgerson Born
description: Knut Torgerson Forthun is born. He will become farm manager of Bnr #8 and marry four wives. His son Ole Knutson will carry the line to America.

title: Patrick O'Brien Born
description: Patrick O'Brien is born in County Clare, Ireland. He will emigrate to Newark, New Jersey, living 'Down Neck' at 43 Broad Street, farming salt marshes.

title: Thomas Olson Forthun Born
description: Thomas Olson Forthun is born at the Forthun farm. He will become the family's great emigrant, leaving Norway for America in 1853.

title: Sarah O'Brien Born
description: Sarah O'Brien is born December 26 in Newark, New Jersey. Her mother may have been a Noonan or Scanlon. She will marry Bernard Sylvester McEntee.

title: Susanna Ottum Emigrates
description: Susanna Johannesdotter Ottum emigrates from Luster, Norway to America. She will marry Thomas Olson Forthun four years later.

title: Thomas Olson Forthun Emigrates
description: Thomas Olson Forthun leaves the Forthun farm and emigrates to America, settling in Franklin County, Wisconsin. Five of his siblings will follow between 1848 and 1856.

title: Thomas & Susanna Marry in Wisconsin
description: Thomas Olson Forthun marries Susanna Johannesdotter Ottum on October 13 in Dane County, Wisconsin. They will have eight children.

title: Hans Thomassen Fortney Born
description: Hans Thomassen Fortney is born February 18 in Vernon County, Wisconsin. The son of Norwegian emigrants, he will become the patriarch of the American Fortney family.

title: Kristina Olsdotter Fortun Born
description: Kristina Olsdotter Fortun is born May 26 at the Forthun farm. She will be the last member of the family to live on Bnr #8, managing it until around 1925.

title: Fortun Stave Church Moved to Bergen
description: The original Fortun Stave Church, which had grown too small for the congregation, is sold to Bergen merchants. It is relocated to Bergen and becomes the famous Fantofte Stavkirke.

title: Hans & Maria Fortney Marry
description: Hans Thomassen Fortney marries Maria Johanna Swangstu on April 6, in Viroqua, Wisconsin. Together they will have ten children and move the family to Wheeler, WI in 1907.

title: Albin Leonard Fortney Born
description: Albin Leonard Fortney is born July 10 in Viroqua, Wisconsin. He will become a Lutheran minister and U.S. Army Chaplain Major, serving in WWII from Pearl Harbor to the liberation of Norway.

title: Anita Linnea Södergren Born
description: Anita Linnea Södergren is born February 17 in Moline, Illinois. The daughter of Rev. Carl Johannes Södergren, she will marry Albin Leonard Fortney in 1935.

title: Fortney Family Moves to Wheeler
description: Hans and Maria Fortney move their family from Viroqua to Wheeler, Wisconsin. Wheeler becomes the family's home base for generations.

title: Kristina Receives Farm Deed
description: On January 29, Kristina Olsdotter Fortun receives the deed to Bnr #8 from her emigrating brother Christen. She will manage the farm until around 1925 — the last Fortney on the ancestral land.

title: Dublin Fire Destroys Irish Records
description: The Four Courts fire in Dublin destroys centuries of Irish genealogical records, making it difficult to trace the pre-emigration history of the Mulhearn, McEntee, and O'Brien families.

title: Albin & Anita Marry
description: Albin Leonard Fortney and Anita Linnea Södergren marry on May 28 in Minneapolis, Minnesota. Together they will have four children and live in 25 homes across nine states and two European countries.

title: Steven Dale Fortney Born
description: Steven Dale Fortney is born January 2 in Minneapolis. Anita traveled from Cook, MN in the dead of winter rather than trust local doctors.

title: Alan Jon Fortney Born
description: Alan Jon Fortney is born November 18 in Rocky River, Ohio — on the very day his father received his commission as a Second Lieutenant in the U.S. Army. His first crib was an opened bureau drawer.

title: Albin Receives Army Commission
description: Albin Leonard Fortney receives his commission as Second Lieutenant in the U.S. Army, beginning a military career that will take his family around the world.

title: Kristina Olsdotter Fortun Dies
description: Kristina Olsdotter Fortun dies January 12 in Forthun — the last member of the Fortney family to live on the ancestral Bnr #8 farm. She never married.

title: Kendall Thomas Fortney Born
description: Kendall Thomas Fortney is born June 2 in Niagara Falls, New York. Named for his great-uncle Thomas Kendahl, he will grow up to serve as an Army medic in Vietnam.

title: Pearl Harbor — Albin in Hawaii
description: Albin Leonard Fortney is in Hawaii on December 7, 1941, when the Japanese attack Pearl Harbor. He is put in charge of burials. His family, scheduled to arrive December 24, must stay in Portland, Oregon instead.

title: Albin Deployed to European Theater
description: Albin Leonard Fortney, now a Major Chaplain, is deployed to the European theater of World War II. The family moves to Menomonie, Wisconsin.

title: Albin in Norway — Operation Midnight Sun
description: Albin Leonard Fortney serves in Norway as part of Operation Midnight Sun, the U.S. Army's liberation of Norway from the Nazis. He visits the parish Bible in Forthun — an official record of the Forthun families.

title: Anita Linnea Fortney Born
description: Anita Linnea Fortney (later Harms) is born July 3 in Tacoma, Washington. Her mother remarked she had four children in four different cities and four different states.

title: Family Posted to Schweinfurt, Germany
description: Albin re-enlists on the condition the whole family can go to Europe. The family crosses the Atlantic in February storms aboard the USS Callan, living at 7 Killiansberg, just below the mental hospital.

title: Family Posted to Vienna, Austria
description: Albin's second European posting. Living at 23 Kassgraben Gasse. He develops heart problems and is sent home for discharge at Carlisle Barracks. The children catch fireflies with their Piepkorn cousins.

title: Kendall Thomas Fortney Killed in Vietnam
description: Army Medic Kendall Thomas Fortney is killed in action at La Cua, Vietnam on February 6. Due to bureaucratic error, he is initially listed as MIA. Senator William Proxmire and journalist Larry K. Knutsen help identify his body; he is buried in Stoughton in March.

title: Albin Leonard Fortney Dies
description: Albin Leonard Fortney dies May 18 of colonic cancer — just 10 days before his 33rd wedding anniversary. He had lived to see his youngest son Kendall buried only weeks before. He was 61 years old.

title: Anita Södergren Fortney Dies
description: Anita Linnea Södergren Fortney dies July 4, before breakfast, in Skaalen Home, Stoughton — after 22 years, 2 months, and 6 days as a widow. She had lived in 25 homes across her life. She was 83 years old.

title: Fantofte Stavkirke Burned
description: The Fantofte Stavkirke in Bergen — originally the Fortun Stave Church from the ancestral village — is burned by arsonist vandals as part of a wave of church arsons in Norway. It is rebuilt by the family that had originally refurbished it.

title: Alan & Jill Visit Forthun Farm
description: Alan Jon Fortney and Jill Andersen Fortney visit the ancestral Forthun farm in Norway. With the help of current owner Torill Magnusdotter Yttri Otterhjell, they verify the genealogy and add new information. Jill photographs the farm, the village, and the kirkegård.

title: Fortney Centennial Reunion
description: The Fortney family holds its centennial reunion on August 4, 2007, commemorating 100 years since Hans & Maria Fortney moved to Wheeler, Wisconsin. A major Keynote presentation documents the Norwegian origins and American family history.
